---
title: "Receptor Scan Metric Agreement (Jaccard Index)"
---

# Summary
Compares top-ranked receptor pairs across any combination of scan metrics. Each metric is
a named (path, column) pair — selectivity, KL divergence, EMD, or any numeric column from
a scan CSV. For each user-specified comparison, the Jaccard index is computed at varying
absolute top-N thresholds across all shared cell types.

Receptor pairs are normalized so (A, B) and (B, A) are treated as identical. Cell type ×
threshold combinations where N exceeds the valid pair count are excluded.

# Inputs
- `METRICS`: dict mapping display label → `{"path": ..., "column": ...}` for each metric
- `COMPARISONS`: list of `(label_a, label_b)` tuples drawn from `METRICS` keys
- `TOP_N_VALUES`: absolute top-N thresholds to evaluate
- `MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT`: exclude cell types with fewer than this % of the max valid pair
  count (0 = keep all, applied per-comparison or globally depending on `GLOBAL_PAIR_INTERSECTION`)
- `GLOBAL_PAIR_INTERSECTION`: if True, restrict every comparison to the single pair pool
  that is valid across **all** metrics and cell types — making Jaccard values directly
  comparable across comparisons; if False, each comparison uses the pairwise intersection
  of its two metrics (larger pools, but values not cross-comparable)
- `SHOW_LINE_PLOT`: if True, render per-cell-type line traces in addition to the box plot
- `SCALE_DOT_SIZE`: if True, dot size scales with the valid pair count for that cell type;
  use False when pair counts are similar across comparisons (e.g., selectivity-only)

# Outputs
- Box plot: Jaccard at each top-N threshold, one box per comparison per group. Dot size
  scales with valid pair count when `SCALE_DOT_SIZE` is True, otherwise fixed.
- Line plot (optional): one panel per comparison, Jaccard vs. top N with per-cell-type
  traces and mean overlay.
- Summary table: mean Jaccard per comparison × threshold.

```{python}
%config InlineBackend.figure_formats = ['svg']
import pandas as pd

# ── Parameters ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Example 1 — selectivity scan vs. selectivity scan (Raw vs. Normalized)
# METRICS = {
#     "Raw counts": {
#         "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_selec_scan_full.csv",
#         "column": "Selectivity",
#     },
#     "Normalized counts": {
#         "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260529_selec_scan_full_expr-matched.csv",
#         "column": "Selectivity",
#     },
# }
# COMPARISONS = [("Raw counts", "Normalized counts")]
# TOP_N_VALUES = [5, 10, 50, 100, 500, 1000]
# MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT = 0    # 0–100, percentage of maximum number of valid pairs required
# SCALE_DOT_SIZE = False   # True = dot size ∝ valid pair count; False = fixed size (use when pair counts are similar across comparisons)

# Example 2 — selectivity scan vs. selectivity scan (varying valencies)
METRICS = {
    "Fixed Bivalent": {
        "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_selec_scan_full.csv",
        "column": "Selectivity",
    },
    "Fixed Tetravalent": {
        "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260529_selec_scan_full_bival.csv",
        "column": "Selectivity",
    },
    "Mixed Tetravalent": {
        "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260608_selec_scan_full_mix-val.csv",
        "column": "Selectivity",
    },
}
COMPARISONS = [
    ("Fixed Bivalent", "Fixed Tetravalent"),
    ("Fixed Bivalent", "Mixed Tetravalent"),
    ("Fixed Tetravalent", "Mixed Tetravalent"),
]
TOP_N_VALUES = [5, 10, 50, 100, 500, 1000]
MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT = 0  # 0–100, percentage of maximum number of valid pairs required
SCALE_DOT_SIZE = False  # True = dot size ∝ valid pair count; False = fixed size (use when pair counts are similar across comparisons)

# Example 3 — selectivity vs. distribution metrics (+ KL vs. EMD)
# METRICS = {
#     "Selectivity": {
#         "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_selec_scan_full.csv",
#         "column": "Selectivity",
#     },
#     "EMD": {
#         "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_dist_scan_full_filt.csv",
#         "column": "EMD",
#     },
#     "KL Divergence": {
#         "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_dist_scan_full_filt.csv",
#         "column": "KL_Divergence",
#     },
# }
# COMPARISONS = [
#     ("Selectivity", "EMD"),
#     ("Selectivity", "KL Divergence"),
#     ("EMD", "KL Divergence"),
# # ]
# TOP_N_VALUES = [5, 10, 50, 100]
# MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT = 10    # 0–100, percentage of maximum number of valid pairs required
# SCALE_DOT_SIZE = True   # True = dot size ∝ valid pair count; False = fixed size (use when pair counts are similar across comparisons)

# Example 4 — multiple selectivity scans vs. distribution metrics
# METRICS = {
#     "Generic signal": {
#         "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_selec_scan_full.csv",
#         "column": "Selectivity",
#     },
#     "CD122 signal": {
#         "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260604_selec_scan_full_sigCD122.csv",
#         "column": "Selectivity",
#     },
#     "CD28 signal": {
#         "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260609_selec_scan_full_sigCD28.csv",
#         "column": "Selectivity",
#     },
#     "EMD": {
#         "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_dist_scan_full_filt.csv",
#         "column": "EMD",
#     },
#     "KL Divergence": {
#         "path": "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_dist_scan_full_filt.csv",
#         "column": "KL_Divergence",
#     },
# }
# COMPARISONS = [
#     ("Generic signal", "EMD"),
#     ("CD122 signal", "EMD"),
#     ("CD28 signal", "EMD"),
#     ("Generic signal", "KL Divergence"),
#     ("CD122 signal", "KL Divergence"),
#     ("CD28 signal", "KL Divergence"),
# ]
# TOP_N_VALUES = [5, 10, 50, 100]
# MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT = 10    # 0–100, percentage of maximum number of valid pairs required
# SCALE_DOT_SIZE = True   # True = dot size ∝ valid pair count; False = fixed size (use when pair counts are similar across comparisons)

GLOBAL_PAIR_INTERSECTION = True  # True = same pair pool for all comparisons
scale = "log"  # None or "log" for line plot x-axis


assert all(m in METRICS for pair in COMPARISONS for m in pair), (
    "All COMPARISONS labels must appear in METRICS."
)

# ── Load unique files once ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_path_to_df = {}
for info in METRICS.values():
    p = info["path"]
    if p not in _path_to_df:
        _path_to_df[p] = pd.read_csv(p)


# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def add_norm_pair(df):
    df = df.copy()
    df["pair"] = [
        tuple(sorted([r1, r2]))
        for r1, r2 in zip(df["Receptor_1"], df["Receptor_2"], strict=False)
    ]
    return df


def jaccard(set_a, set_b):
    union = set_a | set_b
    return len(set_a & set_b) / len(union) if union else 0.0


# ── Build per-metric, per-cell-type series ────────────────────────────────────
metric_series = {}
for label, info in METRICS.items():
    df = _path_to_df[info["path"]]
    col = info["column"]
    ct_map = {}
    for ct in df["Cell_Type"].unique():
        s = (
            df[df["Cell_Type"] == ct][["Receptor_1", "Receptor_2", col]]
            .dropna()
            .pipe(add_norm_pair)
            .groupby("pair", as_index=False)[col]
            .max()
            .set_index("pair")[col]
        )
        if len(s) > 0:
            ct_map[ct] = s
    metric_series[label] = ct_map

comp_labels = [f"{a} vs. {b}" for a, b in COMPARISONS]

# ── Determine valid pair pool per cell type ───────────────────────────────────
comp_ct_data = {}  # comp_label -> {ct -> (series_a, series_b, n_common)}

if GLOBAL_PAIR_INTERSECTION:
    # Restrict to cell types present in every metric, pairs valid in every metric.
    all_cts = sorted(set.intersection(*[set(metric_series[lbl]) for lbl in METRICS]))
    global_pool = {}
    for ct in all_cts:
        common = set.intersection(
            *[set(metric_series[lbl][ct].index) for lbl in METRICS]
        )
        if len(common) < 10:
            continue
        global_pool[ct] = (
            {
                lbl: metric_series[lbl][ct][metric_series[lbl][ct].index.isin(common)]
                for lbl in METRICS
            },
            len(common),
        )

    if global_pool:
        n_max = max(v[1] for v in global_pool.values())
        min_pairs = n_max * MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT / 100
        global_pool = {ct: v for ct, v in global_pool.items() if v[1] >= min_pairs}
        print(
            f"GLOBAL_PAIR_INTERSECTION=True, MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT={MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT}%  →  "
            f"threshold: {min_pairs:.0f} pairs (max across cell types: {n_max})"
        )
    else:
        print("No cell types with ≥ 10 valid pairs across all metrics.")

    print(f"Cell types retained: {sorted(global_pool)}")
    for (metric_a, metric_b), comp_label in zip(COMPARISONS, comp_labels, strict=False):
        comp_ct_data[comp_label] = {
            ct: (data[metric_a], data[metric_b], n)
            for ct, (data, n) in global_pool.items()
        }

else:
    # Each comparison independently intersects its two metrics.
    for (metric_a, metric_b), comp_label in zip(COMPARISONS, comp_labels, strict=False):
        cts = sorted(set(metric_series[metric_a]) & set(metric_series[metric_b]))
        ct_map = {}
        for ct in cts:
            s_a = metric_series[metric_a][ct]
            s_b = metric_series[metric_b][ct]
            common = set(s_a.index) & set(s_b.index)
            if len(common) < 10:
                continue
            ct_map[ct] = (
                s_a[s_a.index.isin(common)],
                s_b[s_b.index.isin(common)],
                len(common),
            )
        if ct_map:
            n_max = max(v[2] for v in ct_map.values())
            min_pairs = n_max * MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT / 100
            ct_map = {ct: v for ct, v in ct_map.items() if v[2] >= min_pairs}
        comp_ct_data[comp_label] = ct_map
        print(
            f"{comp_label}: {len(ct_map)} cell types "
            f"(MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT={MIN_VALID_PAIRS_PCT}%)"
        )

# ── Compute Jaccard ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
records = []
for comp_label, ct_map in comp_ct_data.items():
    for ct, (s_a, s_b, n_total) in ct_map.items():
        for top_n in TOP_N_VALUES:
            if top_n > n_total:
                continue
            records.append(
                {
                    "cell_type": ct,
                    "top_n": top_n,
                    "comparison": comp_label,
                    "jaccard": jaccard(
                        set(s_a.nlargest(top_n).index),
                        set(s_b.nlargest(top_n).index),
                    ),
                    "n_pairs": n_total,
                }
            )

results = pd.DataFrame(records)
if not results.empty:
    print(
        f"Valid pairs range (retained): {results['n_pairs'].min()}–{results['n_pairs'].max()}"
    )
```

```{python}
# ── Box plot: one box per (comparison, top_n) ─────────────────────────────────
import matplotlib.lines as mlines
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from matplotlib.patches import Patch

n_comp = len(comp_labels)
_BASE_COLORS = ["#3A86FF", "#FF9F1C", "#E63946"]
_extra_colors = [plt.cm.tab10(i) for i in range(len(_BASE_COLORS), 10)]
_palette = _BASE_COLORS + _extra_colors
comp_colors = dict(zip(comp_labels, _palette[:n_comp], strict=False))

n_thresh = len(TOP_N_VALUES)
group_width = 0.80
box_width = group_width / n_comp * 0.85
offsets = np.linspace(
    -group_width / 2 + box_width / 2,
    group_width / 2 - box_width / 2,
    n_comp,
)

DOT_SIZE_FIXED = 40
N_SIZE_MIN, N_SIZE_MAX = 20, 220
n_global_min = results["n_pairs"].min() if not results.empty else 0
n_global_max = results["n_pairs"].max() if not results.empty else 1


def pair_count_to_size(n):
    if n_global_max == n_global_min:
        return (N_SIZE_MIN + N_SIZE_MAX) / 2
    return N_SIZE_MIN + (N_SIZE_MAX - N_SIZE_MIN) * (n - n_global_min) / (
        n_global_max - n_global_min
    )


rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(max(8, 2.5 * n_thresh), 5))

for comp_idx, (comp_label, color) in enumerate(comp_colors.items()):
    for thresh_idx, top_n in enumerate(TOP_N_VALUES):
        x_pos = thresh_idx + offsets[comp_idx]
        subset = results[
            (results["comparison"] == comp_label) & (results["top_n"] == top_n)
        ]
        data = subset["jaccard"].values
        sizes = (
            pair_count_to_size(subset["n_pairs"].values)
            if SCALE_DOT_SIZE
            else DOT_SIZE_FIXED
        )

        if len(data) == 0:
            continue

        ax.boxplot(
            data,
            positions=[x_pos],
            widths=box_width,
            patch_artist=True,
            medianprops=dict(color="black", linewidth=1.8),
            whiskerprops=dict(color=color, linewidth=1.0),
            capprops=dict(color=color, linewidth=1.0),
            flierprops=dict(marker=""),
            boxprops=dict(facecolor=color, alpha=0.4, edgecolor=color, linewidth=1.0),
        )
        jitter = rng.uniform(-box_width * 0.28, box_width * 0.28, size=len(data))
        ax.scatter(
            np.full(len(data), x_pos) + jitter,
            data,
            s=sizes,
            color=color,
            alpha=0.75,
            zorder=3,
            linewidths=0.6,
            edgecolors="white",
        )

comp_handles = [
    Patch(facecolor=comp_colors[lbl], alpha=0.55, edgecolor=comp_colors[lbl], label=lbl)
    for lbl in comp_labels
]
comp_legend = ax.legend(
    handles=comp_handles,
    fontsize=10,
    framealpha=0.9,
    loc="upper left",
    title="Comparison",
)
if SCALE_DOT_SIZE:
    ax.add_artist(comp_legend)
    size_legend_vals = [n_global_min, (n_global_min + n_global_max) // 2, n_global_max]
    size_handles = [
        mlines.Line2D(
            [],
            [],
            marker="o",
            color="gray",
            linestyle="None",
            markersize=np.sqrt(pair_count_to_size(n)),
            label=f"n = {n:,}",
        )
        for n in size_legend_vals
    ]
    ax.legend(
        handles=size_handles,
        fontsize=9,
        framealpha=0.9,
        loc="upper right",
        title="Valid pairs (n)",
    )

subtitle = (
    "(each point = one target cell type; size ∝ valid pair count)"
    if SCALE_DOT_SIZE
    else "(each point = one target cell type)"
)
ax.set_xticks(range(n_thresh))
ax.set_xticklabels([f"Top {n}" for n in TOP_N_VALUES])
ax.set_xlabel("Top N receptor pairs", fontsize=13)
ax.set_ylabel("Jaccard Index", fontsize=13)
ax.set_title(
    f"Agreement between top-ranked receptor pairs across scan metrics\n{subtitle}",
    fontsize=11,
)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
ax.grid(True, axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.35)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
# ── Line plot: per-cell-type traces + mean, one panel per comparison ──────────
ct_list = sorted(results["cell_type"].unique())
ct_colors = plt.cm.tab20(np.linspace(0, 1, len(ct_list)))
ct_color_map = dict(zip(ct_list, ct_colors, strict=False))

n_cols = min(n_comp, 3)
n_rows = int(np.ceil(n_comp / n_cols))
fig, axes = plt.subplots(
    n_rows,
    n_cols,
    figsize=(5.5 * n_cols, 4.5 * n_rows),
    sharey=True,
    squeeze=False,
)

for panel_idx, (comp_label, color) in enumerate(comp_colors.items()):
    ax = axes[panel_idx // n_cols][panel_idx % n_cols]
    comp_data = results[results["comparison"] == comp_label]

    for ct in ct_list:
        ct_trace = (
            comp_data[comp_data["cell_type"] == ct]
            .set_index("top_n")["jaccard"]
            .dropna()
            .sort_index()
        )
        if ct_trace.empty:
            continue
        ax.plot(
            ct_trace.index,
            ct_trace.values,
            color=ct_color_map[ct],
            linewidth=1.3,
            alpha=0.65,
            marker="o",
            markersize=4,
            label=ct,
        )

    means = comp_data.groupby("top_n")["jaccard"].mean().sort_index()
    ax.plot(
        means.index,
        means.values,
        color="black",
        linewidth=2.5,
        marker="o",
        markersize=7,
        zorder=5,
        label="Mean",
    )

    if scale == "log":
        ax.set_xscale("log")
    ax.set_xlabel("Top N receptor pairs", fontsize=11)
    ax.set_title(comp_label, fontsize=11, color=color)
    ax.set_xticks(TOP_N_VALUES)
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
    ax.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.35)
    if panel_idx % n_cols == 0:
        ax.set_ylabel("Jaccard Index", fontsize=11)

for panel_idx in range(n_comp, n_rows * n_cols):
    axes[panel_idx // n_cols][panel_idx % n_cols].set_visible(False)

handles, labels_leg = axes[0][0].get_legend_handles_labels()
fig.legend(
    handles,
    labels_leg,
    loc="lower center",
    ncol=min(len(ct_list) + 1, 8),
    fontsize=8.5,
    framealpha=0.9,
    bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.12),
)
plt.suptitle(
    "Scan metric agreement by target cell type across top N receptor pairs",
    fontsize=12,
    fontweight="bold",
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
# ── Summary table: mean Jaccard per comparison × threshold ────────────────────
summary = (
    results.groupby(["top_n", "comparison"])["jaccard"]
    .mean()
    .unstack("comparison")
    .round(3)
)
summary.index = [f"Top {n}" for n in summary.index]
summary.index.name = "Threshold"
print(summary.to_string())
```
